what_it_does: Stores a ledger of named design-pattern findings extracted from pitch decks and landing pages, each entry recording an observed visual or narrative pattern, a recipe, evidence, a prescriptive generator rule, source, depth, and an emergent flag.
observations: Each entry is a reusable design-grammar rule derived from observed slide or page instances.
The majority of entries concern sequencing, attention direction, and visual hierarchy rather than static styling.
A single amber/gold accent is treated as the entire call-to-action and aspiration signal across all sources.
Entries span multiple artifacts and compression levels: 1-page, 15-page, 18-page, 30-page decks, and landing pages.
The emergent boolean distinguishes patterns that surfaced from review versus patterns that appear intentionally designed.
Evidence is mostly slide or page references such as p-02, p24, lp2 strip_02, with occasional free-text inference.
Generator rules are highly prescriptive, often specifying color hex codes, type sizes, positions, counts, and layout percentages.
Several entries explicitly address compression invariance: slides are atomic units to be omitted, not simplified.
standouts: Gold is the sole attention signal - every look-here cue routes through one hue.
Connective-clause title grammar - slide titles read as clauses in one continuous spoken sentence.
Appendix as embedded second deck - the appendix is an alternative self-contained pitch, not mere supplementary material.
Compression-invariance principle - a slide's internal density stays constant; compression happens by omitting whole slides.
Audience-variant grammar - layout mode changes by audience intent, e.g., comparison tables for architects versus chevron journey bars for lifestyle audiences.
conventions: JSON array of objects with a consistent schema: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent, generator_rule, _src, _depth.
Uses snake_case keys including underscore-prefixed metadata fields.
Categories group patterns by concern: sequence, attention-mechanism, attention-direction, attention-verbs, emergent-attention-currency, emergent-attention-directing.
Each finding pairs observation with a prescriptive generator_rule.
Evidence is cited as slide or page identifiers rather than pixel measurements or asset hashes.
concerns: Some evidence strings are vague or inferred rather than tied to concrete visual data.
_src and _depth are free-text strings, not normalized identifiers, which may complicate querying across artifacts.
Color and size prescriptions are hard-coded without references to a shared design-token file.
Overlapping rules from different sources, such as multiple golden-numeral guidelines, are not explicitly reconciled.
No explicit relationships link related findings, e.g., open/close bookend pairs or promoted diagrams.
notes: A pattern library of design grammar rules extracted from reviewed decks and pages, intended to drive generative layout and attention decisions.
questions: How is the emergent versus non-emergent classification validated?
What mechanism maps slide/page references to actual design assets?
Are generator rules applied automatically or curated manually before generation?
How are overlapping or conflicting rules from different sources resolved?
Is there a canonical color-token or type-scale file that these hex and px values reference?
apparent_themes: Compression and scalability across deck depths
Repetition and variation as argumentative devices
intention_signals: It tries to convert subjective design review observations into reproducible generator rules for an automated design system, treating pitch decks and landing pages as a formal grammar with atoms, rules, variants, and compression levels.
novelty: It treats pitch-deck design as a rule-based language, including emergent patterns, compression invariance, and audience-variant grammar, rather than as a set of one-off templates.
contribution: Provides a structured, prescriptive pattern vocabulary - recipes, generator rules, evidence, and source/depth metadata - that a fused generative design system can use to reproduce layout, attention, and sequencing decisions across formats and audiences.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON ledger of named design-pattern findings extracted from pitch decks and landing pages, each documenting an observed visual or narrative pattern, its recipe, evidence, a generator rule, source, depth, and whether it is emergent.
intention_for_embedding: To capture reusable design grammar rules from reviewed artifacts so a generative system can reproduce attention, sequence, and layout patterns consistently across formats and audiences.
